stem: Minimize \( C(x)=x+\dfrac{[[k]]}{x} \) for \( x>0 \). What is the minimum value?
A rectangle has perimeter \( [[P]] \). Using calculus to maximize the area subject to this fixed perimeter, what is the maximum possible area?
solution: With sides \( x \) and \( \frac{[[P]]}{2}-x \), area \( A(x)=x\!\left(\frac{[[P]]}{2}-x\right) \); \( A'(x)=0 \) at \( x=\frac{[[P]]}{4} \), giving a square and maximum area \( \left(\frac{[[P]]}{4}\right)^{2}=\frac{[[P]]^{2}}{16}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops a factor of 2 in the denominator.
This is a single side length, not an area.
This uses the wrong denominator (forgetting to square correctly).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: An open-top box with a square base of side \( x \) and height \( h \) is built to hold a fixed volume \( V=[[V]] \), so \( h=\dfrac{V}{x^{2}} \). Its surface area is \( S(x)=x^{2}+\dfrac{4V}{x} \) for \( x>0 \). What is the minimum possible surface area?
solution: \( S'(x)=2x-\dfrac{4V}{x^{2}}=0 \Rightarrow x^{3}=2V \Rightarrow x=(2V)^{1/3} \); substituting back, the minimum surface area is \( 3(2V)^{2/3}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the leading factor of 3.
This uses 2 instead of 3 as the leading factor.
This uses the wrong exponent (the optimal \( x \), not \( S \) at that \( x \)).
This drops the factor of 2 inside the cube root.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: If \( x+y=[[s]] \) with \( x,y \) real, what is the minimum possible value of \( x^{2}+y^{2} \)?
solution: Substituting \( y=[[s]]-x \), \( f(x)=x^{2}+([[s]]-x)^{2} \) has \( f'(x)=0 \) at \( x=\dfrac{[[s]]}{2} \), giving \( x=y=\dfrac{[[s]]}{2} \) and minimum value \( \dfrac{[[s]]^{2}}{2}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the classic maximum-product value \( xy \), not the minimum sum of squares.
This forgets to divide by 2 at all.
This is just \( x=y \) itself, not \( x^{2}+y^{2} \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
